item.text: I’ve confirmed the validator’s search complaint in the current code path. I’m cutting Postgres search over to `full_text_index` only and adding the negative metadata-token assertion now, before I do the product-file cleanup pass.

The search fix and negative assertion are patched. I’m doing the packet-cleanup pass again now with the same reversible safety pattern as before, but only for out-of-scope product files so the remaining diff is Loom-only.

item.text:
**Fixes**
The validator was right about PostgreSQL search. In [storage/postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs#L2337), Loom search no longer matches raw `derived_json`; it now searches only the same portable surface SQLite exposes: `title`, `original_filename`, and `derived_json::jsonb ->> 'full_text_index'`. I also added the missing negative conformance check in [storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L1117) to prove a metadata-only token (`metadata_shadow`) does not match on either backend, and kept the paged sorted-view parity assertion at [storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs#L1060).

**Verification**
Commands run and results:
- `cargo fmt -- src/loom_fs.rs src/storage/postgres.rs src/storage/sqlite.rs src/storage/tests.rs tests/storage_conformance.rs` -> completed
- `cargo test -p handshake_core loom` -> passed; 6 Loom unit/API/path/migration tests passed, plus 2 Loom storage conformance tests passed under the filter
- `cargo test -p handshake_core --test storage_conformance` -> passed; 4/4 tests passed
- `git push origin HEAD:refs/heads/feat/WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1` -> branch backup confirmed up to date before cleanup
- `git stash push -m "SAFETY: before Loom packet-clean restore 3" -- <13 out-of-scope product files>` -> safety snapshot created
- `git status --short -- src/backend/handshake_core` -> only packet-scoped Loom files remained modified
- `git status --short -- src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/jobs.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/mcp/gate.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/model_session_scheduler_tests.rs` -> no output; validator-cited out-of-scope examples are clean
- `git stash list --max-count=4` -> safety stashes preserved as `stash@{0}` / `stash@{1}` / `stash@{2}`

**Scope**
Product-code packet cleanliness is restored. The only remaining modified product files are packet-scoped Loom files: [src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs), [src/backend/handshake_core/src/loom_fs.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/loom_fs.rs), [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/loom.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/loom.rs), [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs), [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/sqlite.rs), [src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/tests.rs), and [src/backend/handshake_core/tests/storage_conformance.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Loom-Storage-Portability-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/storage_conformance.rs). Workflow-owned `.GOV/**` files are still active in this orchestrator-managed session; I did not modify them.
